title: Automate source-change watcher (hash-diff vs. snapshots)
context: oncology-glossary-multilingual's allow-listed sources can change over time as oncology terminology and license terms evolve. The manual watcher (watcher-000) requires human initiation. This task automates the source-change watcher so it runs on a schedule or CI trigger, recomputes hashes, and files an issue or fails CI when drift is detected, ensuring the allow-list and definitions stay current.
objective: Implement an automated source-change watcher that periodically re-fetches each allow-listed source, recomputes its SHA-256 hash, compares it to the stored snapshotHash, and files a GitHub issue or CI annotation when drift is detected — building on watcher-000 and the schema from schema-001.
acceptanceCriteria: tooling/watch-sources.js (or equivalent) is updated or replaced to run automatically via GitHub Actions schedule or CI hook rather than only manually

On hash mismatch, the watcher files a GitHub issue or creates a CI annotation identifying the source name, URL, stored hash, and new hash, triggering manual re-verification before further definitions are drafted from that source
The watcher uses the snapshotHash values in sources/term-source-allow-list.yaml (from sources-001) as its reference
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass on the PR
No automatic allow-list updates are made; drift is flagged for human re-verification only
output: PR replacing or extending watcher-000 with an automated scheduled source-change watcher
